message: fix(guard,tools,server): case-insensitive path handling for macOS/Windows and XSS prevention in terminal markdown Cross-platform correctness fixes for file path conflict detection and reservation indexing on case-insensitive filesystems, plus security hardening for terminal markdown rendering. Case-Insensitive Path Handling: - guard/lib.rs: GlobBuilder now enables .case_insensitive(true) when the ignorecase flag is set, making path conflict detection respect macOS/Windows filesystem semantics - reservation_index.rs: new routing_key() helper lowercases strings on macOS and Windows (compile-time cfg!) and leaves them as-is on Linux; all hash map lookups (exact paths, ancestor paths, descendant prefixes, glob prefixes) now go through routing_key() so Src/Main.rs and src/main.rs are treated as the same path on case-insensitive filesystems - reservations.rs: relativize_path() now iterates part-by-part using eq_ignore_ascii_case on Windows and exact comparison on other platforms, fixing path relativization when project root and input path differ only in case Terminal Markdown XSS Prevention (tui_markdown.rs): - New attribute_filter on TERMINAL_MARKDOWN_SANITIZER blocks data: URIs in href and src attributes, with an exception for data:image/* on tags (excluding SVG which can contain scripts) - Prevents injection of data:text/html payloads through OSC 8 hyperlinks in terminal output Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context)
